chunks.text: Course and Instructional Support - EECS at Berkeley Course and Instructional Support Course Support Contacts ee-scheduling@eecs.berkeley.edu EE class scheduling in Cory Hall cs-scheduling@eecs.berkeley.edu CS class scheduling in Soda Hall. cs-enrollments@eecs.berkeley.edu Questions about enrolling in CS classes. eecs-course-support@eecs.berkeley.edu All administrative course support questions and requests (copying, textbooks, eGrade submissions, etc.) eecs-course-management@eecs.berkeley.edu Management of undergraduate student instructors and courses. inst@eecs.berkeley.edu Instructional computing (class accounts, instructional websites) Labs and Classroom Facilities Cory and Soda Halls Room scheduling: for semester-length seminars, classes, labs, sections, TA/GSI office hours, and exams, contact ee-scheduling@eecs.berkeley.edu or cs-scheduling@cs.berkeley.edu Instructional labs A/V resources Campus Classrooms: capacities & resources / technology support Special accommodations for disabled students Teaching and Class Materials EE and CS Course Support : textbooks, class readers, photocopying, etc., contact eecs-course-support@eecs.berkeley.edu EECS Instructors : course tools, software, accounts, class rosters and home pages EECS grading guidelines : graduate / undergraduate Course instructors are contacted directly when the grades submission system opens. Undergraduate teaching objectives and outcomes . Disposition of final exams : Graded final exams must be kept on file on campus for thirteen months, so please arrange with course support staff to have them stored where they are available if students wish to review them. Students may look at their graded exams under supervision, but not leave with or photocopy them. However, course support staff can make a copy for them. Put books on course reserve at the Kresge Engineering Library Campus teaching policies : attendance, course approval, evaluations, ethics Resources Information for Current Students Broadening Participation
